summary: Intraoperative histopathology provides quick results useful during surgeries like Mohs or certain biopsies; it is capable of delivering near-definitive diagnoses promptly based on reviewed literature supporting various surgical applications where timely tissue assessment impacts treatment decisions significantly. However, there might be nuances not covered by general statements suggesting absolute definitiveness every time due to variability depending upon specific conditions encountered clinically. To pinpoint which statement among options A through E isn't universally accurate would require examining each one closely against current medical knowledge encompassing both common practices using such techniques effectively along with rare exceptions showing limitations despite overall utility described here broadly applicable yet context-specifically variable at times within broader field practice guidelines endorsed medically today. Agent1 concludes their summary focusing primarily on validating core aspects related directly back towards original premise while also hinting cautiously toward potential caveats inherent when
